meta.note: All numbers extracted from the real result TSVs. Narrative lives in content.ts.

provenance.model: developing-brain ChromBPNet, fetal OPC (Trevino 2021 cluster c15)
provenance.cohort: OpenPedCan H3 K27M DMG, 152 patients, somatic non-coding SNVs in OPC-accessible c15 peaks
sources: ChromBPNet fetal-OPC model (Trevino 2021 developing-brain multiome, cluster c15)
Zoonomia cactus241way phyloP (Christmas, Sullivan et al., Science 2023)
lentiMPRA + caQTLs (Deng, Whalen ... Pollard, Science 2024, adh0559)

superEnhancer.motifFrac: 4 of 29 disrupt a clear motif at >=50% (6 of 29 at >=30%)

confoundTest.note: Confound-hardened (A2, Claude Science): the premium survives distance-to-TSS and replication-timing control. Matched-permutation p=0.0010 (obs 0.281 vs matched-null 0.154 [0.114, 0.192]); adjusted median +0.087 [0.021, 0.156], p=2.4e-3; confounds explain <10%. Replication timing predicts SE membership (p=6e-133) but not phyloP (rho=0.002), so it cannot mediate. Effect modest; rep-timing track a cross-cell proxy.

crossCheck.note: Independent cross-check (A5, AlphaGenome): agrees with NCypher's chromatin direction on 82% of variants (survives), but not magnitude (threshold-hostage). It does NOT corroborate NPAS3 (generic tracks see a sub-background, opposite-sign effect), so NPAS3 is a top nomination, not a cross-confirmed hit. Best-confirmed hits: SYN3, POLR2F, GDPD5, FARP1. Why the lead disagrees: AlphaGenome has no fetal-OPC/DMG track and cannot represent the K27M-rewired chromatin state, which reinforces the right-cell-context USP.

axisDecomp.note: Conservation-baseline test (A7): the chromatin and constraint axes are statistically independent (Spearman rho=-0.004), so convergence is genuine multi-evidence, not conservation counted twice, answering the sharpest ML-genomics critique. Honest other half: the neurodevelopmental focus is conservation-driven (constraint 2.64x p=1.3e-13; chromatin alone null 1.20x p=0.37). The chromatin axis adds an orthogonal precision filter, mechanism and context-specificity, not the neural biology.

targetLinking.note: ABC target-gene linking (A9): without a matched Hi-C map the method reduces to distance (ABC-top = nearest-TSS 85%), so it does NOT out-resolve nearest-gene, reported honestly as such. One well-supported call: the LINC01117-resident variant most likely regulates LINC01116 (element 306 bp from LINC01116's TSS vs 143 kb from LINC01117's; 10 brain eQTLs p=2e-20), carried by the eQTL line not by ABC. COL1A1 does not re-target (park stands); NPAS3's long-range target is unresolved by ABC alone, later confirmed by real Hi-C (A9b). Note: host-gene labels are provisional (only 43/164 are the nearest TSS).
targetLinking.hicUpgrade: A9b upgrade (Claude Science + Modal): real matched fetal-brain Hi-C (Won et al., 2016) confirmed NPAS3's 854 kb enhancer-to-promoter loop, obs/exp 5.6 at the 99.8th percentile vs distance-matched pairs. The loop pins WHICH gene, not how big the effect, and Hi-C did not cleanly reassign the other targets (skew to structural hubs).

contextTest.note: Multi-context specificity (A3, Modal): re-scored the 164 converged hits through four developing-brain contexts (fetal-OPC c15 + oligo-IPC, early/late radial glia) and a non-neural fetal-heart control. The signal is genuinely NEURAL, not a generic accessibility artefact: unselected-progenitor minus heart median gap +0.208 [0.160, 0.248], one-sided Wilcoxon p=4.9e-24, 90% of variants stronger in brain (control median |log2FC| 0.09 vs 0.23-0.27 in brain). Honest refinement: it is a developing-brain-PROGENITOR signal shared across OPC and radial-glia/IPC lineages, NOT OPC-exclusive (the unselected progenitors fire almost as strongly as OPC). NPAS3 is a clean lead (silent in control, 0.01); SYN3 fires everywhere including heart (0.66), so it is flagged broad, not context-specific.

motifRediscovery.verdict: real TF grammar, not a GC shortcut
motifRediscovery.note: Attribution rediscovery (A4, Modal): DeepSHAP over the top 2,000 c15 peaks, then TF-MoDISco de-novo motif discovery, then TOMTOM vs JASPAR 2024. The model rediscovered the OPC lineage grammar from sequence alone: the SOX HMG box is 43.5% of all seqlet support (the single largest motif; top match SOX4, family-level, SOX10 among matches), the proneural bHLH E-box (CAGCTG) matches OLIG2 (q=1.2e-2), and ETS (ELK1) is present. The neural grammar (SOX + E-box) is 47.7% of support and LEADS the vocabulary. GC-shortcut guard passes: seqlet-weighted GC 0.539 vs 0.486 background, the dominant SOX grammar is AT-rich (0.435, below background), 96.6% of support is sequence-specific, and no pattern is a homopolymer. This closes ML-pitfall 6 (over-interpreting attributions): the model learned biology, not a shortcut. Honest limits: TF identity is family-level not paralogue-level (SOX10 vs SOX4 unresolvable by motif); NFI was not recovered at this scale; contributions are projected not hypothetical DeepSHAP.
